profiles.purpose: Local Apple Silicon MLX machine for executor smoke training, short training runs, eval-pack execution, checkpoint restore rehearsal, export inspection, and CPU-validation ownership.

run_type_admissions.detail: This is the primary admitted machine for MLX smoke runs and local bring-up validation.
Decision-grade MLX runs are allowed only when the question is explicitly MLX-local and the frozen pack or subset is declared before launch.
The Mac profile does not count as the CUDA smoke lane.
The Mac profile does not count as the 4080 decision-grade lane.
The Mac profile does not count as the 4080 confirmation lane.
The Mac profile is a primary owner for CPU-matrix replacement validation, export inspection, and restore rehearsal.
The local Mac profile does not count as the H100 escalation lane.

local_requirements: Run the retained MLX bring-up gate before roadmap-tracked smoke or decision-grade work.
Keep the run local-first: this profile does not claim remote checkpoint-writer or remote cluster-port authority by itself.
Use only shipped Psionic entrypoints when the profile is cited in roadmap-tracked evidence.
profiles.checkpoint_expectations: Checkpoint restore rehearsal and export inspection must stay inside the local operator-owned workspace; this profile does not claim shared checkpoint-writer authority by itself.
connectivity_expectations: No remote launch or cluster-port claim is inherited from the Mac profile alone.
The profile remains valid for local eval and control-plane ownership even when the later Tailnet worker is offline.

authority_artifacts.detail: Retained MLX bring-up report proving the bounded Mac Metal backend, machine envelope, and same-node bring-up gate.
Shared compute-source contract proving the Mac can be admitted under the existing cross-provider train substrate without inventing a second launcher or machine vocabulary.

profiles.claim_boundary: This profile admits the local Apple Silicon MLX machine as a real roadmap-tracked executor development and eval host. It proves local MLX smoke, short-run, eval, restore, export, and CPU-validation posture. It does not by itself claim remote launch, shared checkpoint authority, or cross-device training closure.

profiles.purpose: Operator-selected RTX 4080 CUDA Tailnet worker for bounded smoke, decision-grade, confirmation, eval, and replay-accounted accelerator work inside the admitted local-first executor lane.

run_type_admissions.detail: The 4080 profile does not count as the MLX smoke lane.
The 4080 profile does not count as the MLX decision-grade lane.
This is the primary admitted accelerator for 4080 smoke runs.
This is the primary admitted accelerator for 4080 decision-grade runs once the frozen pack and artifact packet are declared before launch.
This is the primary admitted accelerator for 4080 confirmation reruns after a decision-grade candidate already clears.
The 4080 lane can participate in cross-check validation, but CPU-matrix ownership still sits with the Mac profile.
The admitted 4080 worker does not count as the H100 escalation lane.

local_requirements: Run the retained RTX 4080 bring-up gate before roadmap-tracked smoke, decision-grade, or confirmation work counts.
Use only the admitted Tailnet operator path and shipped Psionic entrypoints; no ad hoc remote wrapper or elastic membership path counts.
Keep the work inside the bounded Rust-only same-node and admitted Tailnet lane; this profile does not widen into dense distributed training.
profiles.checkpoint_expectations: Worker-local scratch may live under the staged remote bundle root such as `$HOME/code/psionic-tailrun/<run_id>/linux`, but retained checkpoints and any roadmap-counting artifact packet must return through the controller-owned bundle under `fixtures/swarm/runs/<run_id>/`.
connectivity_expectations: Tailnet SSH reachability to `archlinux` is required before the 4080 worker profile is considered admitted for a counted run.
The controller-selected coordinator and contributor ports must remain explicit and operator-owned; this profile does not claim wider-network discovery or autonomous launch authority.
If the Tailnet worker is offline, the profile remains documented but does not count for smoke, decision-grade, or confirmation evidence until the admitted path is reachable again.

authority_artifacts.detail: Retained Linux RTX 4080 bring-up report proving the CUDA-labeled worker contract, inventory, and bounded contributor harness.
Shared compute-source contract freezing the admitted RTX 4080 workstation under the existing cross-provider train substrate.
Retained Tailnet admitted-run summary proving the Mac-plus-4080 device set and the current worker contribution path.
Retained short-run device audit recording the current honest 4080 same-node throughput band and its current comparison against the local M5 lane.

throughput_band.detail: The retained 2026-03-27 short-run audit freezes the honest current 4080 same-node band: 82.4025 steps/s before the retained fix and 122.8920 steps/s after it.

profiles.claim_boundary: This profile admits one reachable RTX 4080 CUDA Tailnet worker as the real local accelerator lane for bounded smoke, decision-grade, confirmation, and replay-accounted eval work. It does not claim dense CUDA training closure, public-worker authority, shared checkpoint-writer authority, or independent promotion authority.

summary: Phase-one executor admitted-profile catalog freezing the local Mac MLX lane and the admitted RTX 4080 Tailnet worker lane before the control-plane profile widens the same catalog.
